sections.paragraph:
fut. יִקְרָא.-

(1) to cry out, to call; κράζειν. (A verb. prop. onomatopoetic; used also of beasts (see קֹרֵא ); compare Gr. κράζω (κραγ), κηρύσσω (κηρυγ); in the German languages charen, to cry out; charo, outcry, weeping; often used of the cry of beasts, like krähen, krächzen; French, crier; Engl. to cry; with a prefixed sibilant, skreian; Swedish, skria, fchreien; with a sibilant added at the end, kreifchen, כָּרַז which see. See Fulda’s German roots, p. 115, 227.) It is used absol. of any kind of cry, even when not articulate; like צָעַק Gen 39:14, וָאֶקְרָא בְּקֹול גָּדֹול “I cried with a loud voice.” LXX. ἐβόησα φωνῇ μεγάλῃ. Verse 15 Gen 39:15, הֲרִימֹותִי קֹולִי וָאֶקְרָא. The words which are cried out aloud, often follow, either immediately, Gen 45:1, וַיִּקְרָא הֹוצִיאוּ וגו׳ “and (Joseph) cried, out, Cause to go out,” etc. Gen 41:43, וַיִּקְרָא לְפָנָיו אַבְרֵךְ. Levit. 13:45 Jud. 7:20 2Sa 20:16 2Ki 11:14 Est 6:9, 11 or with the insertion of לֵאמֹר Eze 9:1 וַיֹּאמֵר 2Sa 18:28: compare 2Ki 18:28, וַיִּקְרָא בְקֹול־גָּדֹולּ יְהוּדִית וַיְדַבֵּר וַיּאֹמֶר “and he cried out in a loud voice in the Jews’ dialect, and spake and said.” Specially-(a) followed by אֶל of pers., to call upon, to call to any one (jemandem zurufen), Jud. 18:23 the express words being added, with לֵאמֹר prefixed, 1Sa 26:14, and וַיּאֹמֶר Jud. 9:54 1Sa 17:8 1Ki 17:11 also followed by עַל of pers., Isa 34:14 (“the demons shall cry to one another”); followed by אַחֲרֵי of pers., to cry after any one, to call him as he goes away, 1Sa 20:37, 38 1Sa 20:38 24:9 . It often is-(b) i.q. to ask aid; especially of God; absol. Psa 4:2, בְּקָרְאִי עֲנֵנִי “when I call, hear me.” Psa 22:3 34:7 69:4 followed by אֶל יְהֹוָה Psa 14:4 28:1 30:9 55:17 61:3 Jud. 15:18 16:28 2Ki 20:11 Hos 7:7 לֵאלֹהִים Psa 57:3 with suff., Psa 17:6 88:10 91:15 . With the addition of עַל of pers., on whose account the aid of God is sought, Deu 15:9.-(c) i.q. κηρύσσειν (by which it often is rendered by the LXX.), as a herald or prophet. Absol., Pro 1:21, “wisdom crieth in the broadways.” Pro 8:1 with an acc., Pro 20:6 Isa 40:6, “the voice said, Cry; but he answered, What shall I cry?” Isa 58:1 Zec 1:14, 17 Zec 1:17followed by עַל of the object, Jon 1:2. The words uttered also follow, Exo 32:5 Jer 2:2 7:2 19:2 51:61 or it is joined with an acc. Zec 7:7 Isa 44:7 Joel 4:9 , קִרְאוּ זאֹת בַּגֹּויִם “proclaim this amongst the nations;” or followed by כִּי Isa 40:2. קָרָא דְרֹור לְ to declare, to announce freedom (to slaves, captives), Jer 34:8 15:17 Isa 61:1. קָרָא צֹום to proclaim a fast (to the people), Jer 36:9 Jon 3:5 Ezr 8:21. From the signification of proclaiming, comes also that of reciting and reading; for this, see No. 4.

(2) to call (καλεῖν rufen), specially-(a) to can any one to oneself, followed by an acc. Gen 27:1 Exo 2:8 1Sa 3:16 Hos 7:11 followed by לְ Gen 20:9 Levit. 9:1 Hos 11:1 Isa 46:11 followed by אֶל Gen 3:9 Exo 3:4 1Sa 3:4. קָרָא אֵלָיו to call to oneself, 2Sa 15:2. Metaph. Pro 18:6, “his mouth calleth for strokes,” i.e. deserves and invites them. Rth 4:11, קְרָא שֵׁם בְּבֵית־לֶחֶם i.e. “call (acquire for thyself) a name in Bethlehem.”-(b) when it refers to many, to call together, followed by an acc. Gen 41:8 followed by a dat. Gen 20:8 39:14 אֶל Gen 49:1. Hence קָרָא עֲצָרָה to convene a holy assembly, Joe 1:14 compare Isa 1:13 a feast, Levit. 23:2 , . קְרֻאֵי הָעֵדָה those called to an assembly, Num 1:16.-(c) to call, i.e. to invite any one to a meal (compare καλεῖν ἐπὶ δεῖπνον), 1Sa 9:13, 22 1Sa 9:22 1Ki 1:9, 19 1Ki 1:19, 41 1Ki 1:41, 49 1Ki 1:49figuratively קָרָא לְשָׁלֹום לְ to invite to make peace, Deu 20:10 Jdg 21:13.-(d) to summon before a judge (καλεῖν, καλεῖν εἰς δίκην), Job 5:1 13:22 . ( 14:15 ); Isa 59:4 (parall. נִשְׁפַּט).-(e) to call out soldiers, Isa 13:3.-(f) to call any one to an office, i.q. בָּחַר to choose, followed by an acc. Isa 49:6 48:15 49:1 51:2 followed by לְ Isa 22:20. In the same sense but more emphatic there is said קָרָא בְשֵׁם פ׳ to call any one by name, Isai. 43:1 45:3 , Isa 22:4compare Exo 31:2.-(g) קָרָא בְשֵׁם יְיָ to call upon the name of God, i.e. to celebrate, to praise God, to implore his aid, Gen 4:26 12:8 Exo 33:19 Psa 79:6 105:1 Isa 64:6 Jer 10:25 Zep 3:9 (compare הִזְכִּיר בְּשֵׁם יְיָ); also ק׳ בְּשֵׁם הַבַּעַל 1Ki 18:26. In the same sense, בְּ being omitted, there is said קָרָא שֵׁם יְיָ (which differs from the phrase No. 1, b), Deu 32:3 Psa 99:6 Lam 3:55. (A different sense occurs in Exo 33:19, where God himself speaks, קָרָאתִי בְשֵׁם יְהֹוָה לְפָנֶיךָ “and I will proclaim by name before thee, Jehovah is present,” sc. that thou mayest know the presence of God Most High, I will myself act as the herald who shall announce the coming of God. Compare Gen 41:49.) More rarely it is-(h) to celebrate persons. Psa 49:12, קָֽרְאוּ בִשְׁמֹותָם “they praise their names” (of the rich). Pro 20:6, יִקְרָא אִישׁ חַסְדֹּו “they celebrate every one his own goodness.” The sense is somewhat different in the following, Isa 44:5, זֶה יִקְרָא בְשֵׁם יַעֲקֹב “this man shall celebrate the name of Jacob,” i.e. shall follow and praise the party of Jacob.

(3) to call, to name, to give a name, fully קָרָא שֵׁם לְ to impose a name on any one, Gr. καλειxν τινά τι (II. v. 306; Od. viii. 550), Gen 26:18 Rth 4:17 Psa 147:4. It is variously construed-(a) followed by an acc. of the name and a dative of the thing on which the name is put. Gen 1:5, וַיִּקְרָא אֱלֹהִים לָאֹור; יֹום verses , 10 31:47 1Sa 4:21 Rth 1:20, 21 Isa 47:1 and frequently.-(b) followed by two acc., Num 32:41 Isa 60:18 but commonly-(c) in this manner, Gen 4:25, וַתִּקְרָא אֶת שְׁמֹו שֵׁת “and she called his name Seth;” 4:26 5:2 , , 29 11:9 19:22 27:36 29:34 .

(4) to recite, to read aloud (from the signification of crying out, see No. 1, fin.) any thing, with an acc., Exo 24:7 Jos 8:34, 35 Jos 8:35 2Ki 23:2 also קָרָא בְסֵפֶר to read what is written in a book (comp. שָׁתָה בְ to drink what is in a vessel), Neh 8:8, 18 9:3 Isa 37:14, seqq.; often with the addition of בְּאָזְנֵי פ׳ Ex. Josh. l.l.c.c. נֶגֶד פּ ׳ Deu 31:11. Hence gener. to read, Deu 17:19 2Ki 5:7 19:14 22:8 Isa 29:11. (Arab قرأ Syr. ܩܪܐܳ to read.)

Niphal נִקְרָא-

(1) to be called, i.e. to bid to come by calling, to be called together, Jer 44:26 Est 3:12 6:1 8:9 . נִקְרָא שֵׁם פּ׳ to celebrate any one’s name, Rth 4:14.

(2) to be called, named. Construed-(a) followed by a dat. of person and thing, to which a name is given. Gen 2:23, לְזאֹת יִקּרֵא אִשָּׁה “she shall be called woman;” 1Sa 9:9 Isa 1:26 32:5 62:4 , 12 Isa 62:12.-(b) with two nominatives. Zec 8:3, נִקְרְאָה יְרוּשָׁלַים עִיר הָאֱמֶת “Jerusalem shall be called the city of truth;” Isa 48:2 54:5 56:7 and-(c) with the addition of the noun שֵׁם. Gen 17:5, לֹא יִקָּרֵא עֹוד אֶת־שִׁמְךָ אֲבְרָם “thy name shall no more be called Abram;” 45:10 Deu 25:10 Dan 10:1. Compare as to this threefold construction in Kal No. 3.

Also observe these phrases-(a) נִקְרָא בְשֵׁם פּ׳ to be called by any one’s name, i.e. to be reckoned to his race, Isa 43:7 48:1 followed by בְּ Gen 21:12 and in like manner עַל שֵׁם Gen 48:6, עַל שֵׁם אֶחָיו יִקָּֽרְאוּ “they shall be counted with their brethren” (shall bear the same name as their brethren, shall be called the sons of Joseph, not of Jacob); also מִן Isa 48:2, מֵעִיר הַקֹּדֶשׁ נִקְרָאוּ i.e. they wish to be called the inhabitants of the city.-(β) נִקְרָא שְׁמִי עַל my name is called upon any thing, i.e. it is added to that thing, the thing is called mine (as the house of one’s father is the house which the father possesses), Isa 4:1 2Sa 12:28. So of the people of Israel who bear the name of God (who are called the people of God), Deu 28:10 Isa 63:19 Jer 14:9 Amo 9:12 2Ch 7:14 used of the temple, 1Ki 8:43 Jer 7:10, 11 , 14 , 30 34:15 Jerusalem, Dan 9:18, 19 prophets, Jer 15:16. Also, to be called, is sometimes used for to be (since men call us, and we acquire cognomens from what we are, or at least, from what we seem to be), as Isa 1:26, “afterward thou shalt be called the city of righteousness,” i.e. thou shalt be graced with such an epithet, because in fact thou shalt be righteous; Isa 9:5 30:7 [both Kal]; 35:8 47:1 , Isa 47:5[both Kal]; 48:8 [Pual]; 56:7 (compare 4:3 19:18 , and my Comment. on Isa., iii. p. 29). So Gr. κεκλῆσθαι Il. iv. 61, Od. vii. 313. Monk ad Eurip. Hippolyt., 2. Porson ad Phœniss., 576.

(3) to be read aloud, recited, Est 6:1 followed by בְּ in a book, Neh 13:1.

Pual-

(1) pass. of Kal No. 2, letter f, to be called, i.e. to be chosen, Isa 48:12.

(2) to be called, named, Isa 65:1 generally, קֹרָא לְ 48:8 58:12 61:3 62:2 Eze 10:13. See also the remarks at the end of Niphal.

Derivatives, קֹרֵא, קָרִיא, קְרִיאָה, מִקְרָא.

II.

קרא

i.q. קָרָה to meet, hence to happen, to occur to any one (whether good or bad), followed by an acc. of pers., Gen 42:4, 38 Gen 42:38 49:1 Lev 10:19. Inf. קִרְאָה (of the form יִרְאָה) a meeting, hence לְקִרְאַת which every where (in the Syriac manner) is contracted into לִקְרַאת, with suff. לִקְרָאתִי, לִקְרַאתְכֶם prep.-

(1) towards, to meet, Gen 46:29 Exo 4:27 18:7 in a hostile sense, Jud. 7:24 pregn., Jos 11:20, “that their hearts should be hardened לִקְרַאת הַמִּלְחָמָה to go into battle.”

(2) opposite to, over against, Gen 15:10 1Sa 4:2.

Niphal-

(1) to be made to meet, to meet any one, followed by עַל Exo 5:3 לִפְנֵי 2Sa 18:9 used of things, Deu 22:6.

(2) to be by chance, to happen, 2Sa 1:6 20:1 .

Hiphil, to cause to happen (evil to any one), with two acc., Jer 32:23.
